purpose: The commit addresses a bug where Bluetooth commands were being queued even after the device was unregistered, leading to a use-after-free (UAF) error. The fix ensures that hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, preventing this issue.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c, as it's already included via linux/tty.h. This simplifies the include structure and reduces redundancy.
The commit updates documentation for a performance tool to reflect changes in store metrics, specifically adding an 'N/A' metric and updating the report table accordingly.

This commit addresses issues in vma lock handling during split operations. It fixes race conditions and improper lock management by ensuring each new vma has its own lock and adjusting how locks are freed during unmapping.
The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies in the rt5682s codec's device tree bindings. These supplies are already used by a specific board but were not properly documented.
The commit addresses a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver's remove function. Specifically, it adds an iounmap() call to properly release resources that were not being freed before.
The commit removes an unused work field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx. This is a code cleanup task aimed at improving code quality by eliminating dead code.
The commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed to prevent warnings when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in RISC-V defconfigs without introducing any functional changes.

The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode configuration in the chip's FIFOs. This improves code maintainability and reduces redundancy.
The commit modifies self-tests related to the RTC hardware. It skips tests when the RTC is not present, preventing test failures on systems without an RTC.

The commit removes a redundant assignment of the variable 'bytes' in the ALSA echoaudio driver. The initial assignment is never read and is later reassigned within an if block, making it unnecessary. This change addresses a clang scan build warning about dead code.
The commit modifies the build configuration to allow rcuscale without requiring RCU Tasks in certain kernel setups. This reduces unnecessary code inclusion and simplifies tracing operations.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in spte.h, improving code clarity.

The commit reverts a previous change that caused issues with clock tables in AMD display drivers. The problem arose because using dcfclk-based clk table led to underflow when certain states were disabled.
This commit adds a compatible string to document the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU in the device tree bindings. It ensures that the Linux kernel recognizes and supports this specific GPU.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with other naming conventions in the DW PCIe core driver.

The commit addresses a clang warning related to incorrect format specifier usage in printf-like functions. The argument type was being promoted, causing a mismatch between the format string and the actual data type.
The commit fixes a bug where the upper bound of a branch range was not properly handled when comparing signed values. By casting it to long, it prevents negative offsets from triggering warnings.
The commit removes an unnecessary case from SetHwReg8188EU() by directly calling the function instead. This is part of cleaning up the hal layer.
This commit fixes sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code, improving code quality and preventing potential linking issues.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings, correcting a misconfiguration.

The commit modifies the allocation of arrays within the struct ctrl_ref to prepare for future driver-defined size changes. This change aims to enhance flexibility and prevent potential issues related to array management.
The commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs in the socinfo driver, expanding the hardware compatibility of the system.

The commit updates the wifi driver rtl8xxxu to correctly recognize all possible chip versions (from A to P). Previously, any version beyond E would be labeled as 'unknown', which this commit fixes by handling all valid chip cuts.
The commit removes a redundant software enable count in the clock handling code for the ux500 platform, improving code maintainability and reducing potential for inconsistencies.

The commit aims to refactor and clean up the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL in the RISC-V mm initialization code. It groups related conditional usages together, moves macros adjacent to their definitions, and consolidates similar cases to reduce redundancy.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to prepare for future I2C node functionality.
This commit improves code comments by making them more descriptive, fixes a typo, and adjusts code formatting to adhere to coding standards.

This commit addresses an issue where the code could enter an infinite loop if it failed to acquire a hardware semaphore during resource release. The fix involves logging an error and exiting instead of looping indefinitely.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing old-style defines with current standards.
The commit removes redundant code related to IS_SWAPFILE since it's always defined, cleaning up unnecessary checks or workarounds.
The commit implements handling for GTP Echo Request messages by sending appropriate Echo Responses. This ensures compliance with the GTP specification.

This commit replaces certain cpumask functions (cpumask_next_*) with more efficient alternatives (cpumask_first_*). It also introduces a new function, cpumask_first_zero(), to handle cases where the existing functionality wasn't sufficient.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver because the firmware no longer supports it, preventing failure and console errors during roaming.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier by introducing a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) that connects the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail when the socket does not support them, similar to how previous patches handled zerocopy send requests.

The commit modifies the kerneldoc-preamble to check for the existence of xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it. This addresses issues where certain distributions might have missing dependencies, leading to build errors when generating PDF documentation.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros without reporting errors, specifically for macros used in switch-case statements within kernel drivers.
This commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers in the DRM subsystem to improve code structure and facilitate easier sharing among drivers without introducing functional changes.
The commit introduces a JPEG decode worker interface to enable three hardware units to run in parallel on the MT8195 platform.
The commit removes redundant VPRINTK() debug statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function in the libata subsystem, as their information is already covered by tracepoints.

The commit introduces support for entering zstate, a low-power mode, during the extended vertical blanking period when using FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. This optimization allows the display to save power by utilizing extra vblank time if it exceeds 5 ms.
The commit fixes a type mismatch causing a CFI failure in the hqd_destroy() callback by correcting the parameter type from enum to uint32_t.
The commit corrects a typo in the device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl by changing 'reset' to 'resets'. It also adds a validation rule to prevent future misuse of properties.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k wireless driver when compiled with GCC 12. The warning indicates that index 32 is accessed in an array of size 10, which is out of bounds.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the crypto sun8i-ss driver to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes code related to fixed file installation and removal within the io_uring subsystem. It moves these functions into the filetable code where they belong and adjusts helper functions to take a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb, improving code structure.
The commit updates media device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes for better independence and proper power-domain management.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and integrates its contents into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, simplifying the configuration by eliminating redundancy.
